Mrs. Rottjakob addressed the issues raised in the probate examiner's notes stating that $4,500.00 was for the vacation and around $5,000.00 was for an electric bike and the accessories, that the family would not have paid for an HVAC system if not for the beneficiary as they would have just dealt with the heat, that the DFIEX fund was the only one that was left out of the petition and that the other funds were mentioned in the petition and authority is provided in the petition. Mrs. Rottjakob also addressed the fee requests stating that the fees cover the tenth, the eleventh and the twelfth accounting period, that most of the work was done in a two-year period and that it was all encompassed in this petition. The Twelfth Account and Report, etc. is under submission. The court advised Mrs. Rottjakob that it will be making some adjustments to the fees for First Republic and to the attorney fees.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Taylor Rottjakob appeared by zoom for First Republic Trust Company (petitioner).

Mrs. Rottjakob addressed the issues raised in the probate examiner's notes regarding the amount First Republic charged for the investment fees, why the trust paid for 100% of the cost for the HVAC unit when others are also living in the house, the costs of the vacations, etc. The Twelfth Account and Report, etc. is continued to June 25, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar May-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Weaver for (petitioner) First Republic Trust Company.

The Court asked if there are any additional time slips that can be filed that would justify the amounts being paid. Mr. Weaver stated that the last notes he received from the examiner were regarding change of venue, that no hourly fees are being requested, that the notes want an itemization of the filing fee request, that he has a declaration to be filed, that in regards to venue Mr. Rubenstein is the trust officer in Los Angeles, that he is one of 4 trust operations personnel, that the remaining 35 personnel are in San Francisco, that the day to day operations (trades, issuing checks) is all done in San Francisco and that requests for disbursements are made to Mr. Rubenstein in Los Angeles who forwards the request to San Francisco. The petition re substituted judgment trust was taken under submission for review of the declaration.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
